title: primula malvacea subsp. rosthornii
intro: Primula malvacea subsp. rosthornii, also known as the Himalayan primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Primula malvacea subsp. rosthornii include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
